input.email_text: Hi, I noticed I was billed $49.99 twice this month for my Pro plan. Can you please refund the extra charge? My account email is the one I'm writing from.

expected.summary: Customer was double-charged for their Pro plan and wants a refund.

test_cases.notes: Clear, unambiguous billing complaint with a specific ask.

input.email_text: The export to CSV button just spins forever and never actually downloads anything. I've tried on Chrome and Firefox, same result.

expected.summary: Customer reports the CSV export button hangs and never completes on multiple browsers.

test_cases.notes: Clear bug report with reproduction detail.

input.email_text: I forgot my password and the reset email never arrives, I've checked spam folder too.

expected.summary: Customer isn't receiving the password reset email, including in spam.

input.email_text: Just wanted to say I really love the new dashboard redesign, great job team!

expected.summary: Customer is giving positive feedback about the new dashboard redesign.

test_cases.notes: Clear positive feedback, no action needed.

input.email_text: Why is my invoice showing a different plan name than what I signed up for? Is this a billing mistake or did something change with my account settings?

expected.summary: Customer sees a plan name mismatch on their invoice and isn't sure if it's a billing error or account change.

test_cases.notes: Ambiguous — could be billing or account. Billing is the primary intent since the complaint centers on the invoice.

expected.summary: Customer cannot log in and says it's urgent.

test_cases.notes: Extremely short, low-context, tests robustness to terse input.

input.email_text: So the 'premium' support I'm paying extra for means waiting 3 days for a reply? Great, love that for me.

expected.summary: Customer is sarcastically complaining about slow premium support response times.

test_cases.notes: Sarcasm test — model may misread tone. Could arguably be billing (paying for premium) but core complaint is service quality/general feedback.

input.email_text: Necesito cancelar mi suscripcion y no se como hacerlo, el boton no aparece en mi cuenta.

expected.summary: Customer wants to cancel their subscription but can't find the cancel button in their account.

test_cases.notes: Non-English input (Spanish). Tests multilingual handling. Core intent is subscription cancellation = billing.

input.email_text: teh app keeps crashign wehn i try too upload a fiel bigger then 10mb, pls fix

expected.summary: Customer's app crashes when uploading files larger than 10MB.

test_cases.notes: Heavy typos, tests robustness to noisy text.

input.email_text: I want to delete my account permanently and also get a refund for this month since I barely used it.

expected.summary: Customer wants to permanently delete their account and requests a refund for the current month.

test_cases.notes: Genuinely dual-category (account deletion + refund). Labeled account since deletion is the primary action requested; refund is secondary. Good case to watch for category drift.

input.email_text: Do you offer a student discount? I'm currently enrolled full time and would love to keep using the tool.

expected.summary: Customer is asking whether a student discount is available.

test_cases.notes: Pricing question, not a complaint — tests that billing isn't only 'something went wrong'.

input.email_text: 2FA codes never arrive via SMS anymore, started about a week ago. I'm on Verizon if that matters.

expected.summary: Customer's 2FA SMS codes stopped arriving about a week ago.

test_cases.notes: Could be argued as account (login-related) or technical (delivery bug). Labeled technical since it's an SMS delivery failure, not account state.

expected.summary: Customer sent a message with no content beyond a greeting.

test_cases.notes: Near-empty input, tests graceful handling of no real content.

input.email_text: My team plan shows 5 seats but only 3 people can log in, the other 2 get an 'invite expired' error.

expected.summary: Two of five team seats can't log in due to an expired invite error.

test_cases.notes: Could be technical (bug) or account (invite/access). Labeled account since it's about access/invites, not app functionality.

input.email_text: This is the third time I'm emailing about the same billing issue and nobody has responded. I'm extremely frustrated and considering canceling.

expected.summary: Customer is frustrated about an unresolved billing issue after multiple follow-ups and is considering canceling.

test_cases.notes: Emotionally charged but core issue is still billing — tests that sentiment doesn't override category.
